question.content: Build the following structural model: a horizontal rigid beam is supported by two vertical steel rods of length l at points A and C on the beam, with AC=a and CB=a, so C is the midpoint of AB. A third vertical steel rod is above the right end B; its upper end is fixed to a rigid top plate, but this rod is shorter than the first two by delta, so after initial assembly there is a vertical gap delta between its lower end and point B of the beam. An axial strain gauge is attached on rod 3. After assembly, the gauge reading is epsilon1. Then a downward vertical load F is applied at B, so rod 3 contacts the beam and carries force; the gauge reading becomes epsilon2. All three rods are linear elastic, with elastic modulus E and cross-sectional area A. Determine delta and F from the two strain readings. Answer format: delta|F, in this order, separated by |, with no explanatory text or equals signs.

step_1.content: Stage I (no external F): equilibrium gives N1=N3, N2=2N1. Compatibility is (delta-Delta_l3)+Delta_l1=2(Delta_l1+Delta_l2). With Delta_li=Ni*l/(E*A), get N1+2N2+N3=delta*E*A/l.

step_4.content: Stage I equilibrium of the rigid beam: from moments, N1=N3 and N2=2N1.

step_5.content: Substitute N2=2N1 and N3=N1 into N1+2N2+N3=delta*E*A/l: 6N3=delta*E*A/l, so N3=delta*E*A/(6l).

step_6.content: From epsilon1=N3/(E*A), substitute N3=delta*E*A/(6l), then delta=6*l/epsilon1 (using the given convention).

step_7.content: Stage II: equilibrium gives N3'=5F/6, and strain gives N3'=E*A*(2*epsilon2-epsilon1).

step_8.content: Set 5F/6=E*A*(2*epsilon2-epsilon1), then F=6*E*A*(2*epsilon2-epsilon1)/5. Final: delta=6*l/epsilon1, F=6*E*A*(2*epsilon2-epsilon1)/5.
